title: Does the manga Evangelion Its A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world
display_title: Does the manga Evangelion Its A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world
wikitext:
[]: Does the manga Evangelion: It's A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world?             Quote Post by Asugran233 » Sun Apr 11, 2021 12:59 am During the recent reviews, I noticed and came up with a theory that may interest you, I analyzed everything (or during the analysis) and I will present all the arguments: 1- Kaworu -In the manga, a character similar to Kaworu appears, and he himself appears at the train station at the end of 3.0 + 1.0 who talks to Rei. You can see at first glance, the similarity of these characters, white skin, gray hair, red eyes (the latter less more) and are of a similar age (I haven't read the manga, so I won't say too much). 2- NGE is fiction -So yes, you have to explain how the main character disguises herself as Evangelion characters, even though Evangelion's world itself has been reborn (if that makes sense), my argument is simple: Since we see Hideaki Anno at the end of the movie, it means that the and EoE (with the difference that there are no rebuilds) are fiction, and these characters like Asuka or Shinji are reincarnations of anime characters who are ... (maybe it sounded nonsense), so yeah ... that's probably what I wanted to say in the point. 3- Other characters -there is also a character that resembles Fuyutsuki, and at the end of the movie we only see Evangelion pilots, it means that other characters had to hit different places. So probably Sakura's boss must be Fuyutsuki. Ritsuko is also somewhere, but I didn't dig into the information. To sum up, the theory is plausible and there is no doubt that the manga Evangelion: It's A Miraculous Win (adds "Evangelion" to it ...) so, how do you like the theory?

[]: Re: Does the manga Evangelion: It's A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Sun Apr 11, 2021 3:40 am I doubt it. It's A Miraculous Win is a spin-off material created mostly, if not entirely due to commercial reasons and I really doubt that Anno would either keep this manga in mind when making Shin or that the people behind it would somehow have knowledge of Shin's ending. The only way this would work is if we were to go all "death of the author" on this, but if that has to be incurred to make sense of a theory, then that theory, unless concerned with themes, probably wasn't that true anyway.

[]: Re: Does the manga Evangelion: It's A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Apr 11, 2021 11:09 am Yeah, this is one of those titles that were made with so little input from anyone at khara that I wouldn't pay too much mind to it. It's in a similar spot as that Minivangelion comic book, for example

[]: Re: Does the manga Evangelion: It's A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 11, 2021 12:50 pm you can have whatever hcs you want of course. But given the sheer amount of wacky stuff we've gotten, i doubt that anyone makes sure that the various spinoffs fit into any sort of unified canon dcu style.

Article: Does the manga Evangelion Its A Miraculous Win take place in a rebuilt world
40 am I doubt it. It's A Miraculous Win is a spin-off material created mostly, if not entirely due to commercial reasons and I really doubt that Anno would either keep this manga in mind when making Shin or that the people behind it would somehow have knowledge of Shin's ending. The only way this would work is if we were to go all "death of the author" on this, but if that has to be incurred to make sense of a theory, then that theory, unless concerned with themes, probably wasn't that true anyway.
